purpose: The clause-map plumbing pinned from both sides on an edge-clause-first rule (friends graph has more edges than nodes, so reason()'s reorder pass runs with a non-identity map): get_rules afterwards shows the reordered live rule (popular(y) moved ahead of Friends(x,y)), while the saved CSV's Clause-1..4 columns are mapped back to the author's original clause order — Clause-1 carries the Friends edge groundings.
provenance: spine case against docs/surface.md fn:save_rule_trace clause-map-reorder + fn:get_rules loaded (post-reason live mutation); reorder_clauses.py:6-30 and output.py:92-97,114-123 at the pin; screened 2026-07-07
